steps.title: Square the equation
steps.content:
Any solution must satisfy $29-x\ge0$. Squaring the equation gives
$$k-x=(29-x)^2$$

steps.title: Create one solution
steps.content:
Expand and move everything to one side.
$$0=x^2-57x+841-k$$
For exactly one real solution, the discriminant must be $0$.
$$57^2-4(1)(841-k)=0$$

steps.title: Solve for the minimum
steps.content:
Simplify the discriminant equation.
$$3{,}249-3{,}364+4k=0$$
$$4k=115$$
The repeated root satisfies $x\le29$, so it is valid. The answer is 115.
